contractScope: slice2 - done, delete, and clear commands

checks.name: done_command_marks_completed
checks.evidence: cmd_done() calls mark_done() which sets task['done'] = True and persists. Confirmation message shown on success.

checks.name: done_status_appears_in_list
checks.evidence: cmd_list() displays '✓' for completed tasks and ' ' for pending. Uses task.get('done') safely.

checks.name: delete_removes_task
checks.evidence: cmd_delete() calls delete_task() which removes task from list and persists. Confirmation message shown.

checks.name: delete_task_not_in_list
checks.evidence: After delete, task no longer appears in list output because it's removed from storage array.

checks.name: clear_requires_confirmation
checks.evidence: cmd_clear() prompts user with input('Delete all completed tasks? (y/N): ') and only executes on 'y'.

checks.name: clear_removes_all_tasks
checks.evidence: clear_completed_tasks() filters out all tasks where done=True and persists. Returns removed count.

checks.name: clear_only_removes_completed
checks.evidence: Filter condition: [task for task in tasks if not task.get('done')]. Pending tasks preserved.

checks.name: error_invalid_task_id
checks.evidence: done/delete validate int conversion with try/except, show 'Error: invalid task ID' on ValueError.

checks.name: error_nonexistent_id
checks.evidence: mark_done/delete return False if not found, CLI shows 'Error: task #X not found' and exits(1).

checks.name: storage_valid_json_after_ops
checks.evidence: All operations use save_tasks() with json.dump(tasks, f, indent=2). Maintains JSON array structure.

checks.name: existing_add_list_unchanged
checks.evidence: cmd_add() and cmd_list() retain original logic. Only list output added status indicator.

checks.name: data_persists_correctly
checks.evidence: mark_done, delete_task, clear_completed_tasks all call save_tasks() after modifications.

maintainabilityNotes: Consistent error handling pattern across done/delete (arg validation -> int parse -> storage call -> success/failure message)
Clear separation: storage layer handles persistence, CLI layer handles I/O and validation
Safe use of task.get('done') handles legacy tasks without 'done' field
Confirmation prompt in clear has sensible default (y/N requires explicit 'y' to proceed)
All commands exit(1) on error with stderr output
acceptanceCriteriaMet: Running 'done <id>' marks the specified task as completed
Running 'list' displays tasks with their completion status
Running 'delete <id>' removes the specified task from storage
Running 'clear' removes all tasks from storage with user confirmation
Data persists correctly after done, delete, and clear operations
Clear error messages shown for invalid task IDs
